reviews.review_msg: Really sweet and easy to talk to professor, and easy class, quizzes and test are purely memorization of dates, people and techniques. Please laugh at his jokes even if you don't understand them.

Extremely professional, friendly, and amazing, well-put-together lectures. He knows how to lecture without only reading what he has on his slides.

He has a policy that restricts electronics use for note-taking, this was annoying at first but led to a very interactive and attentive environment during lectures. A definite positive.

This professor is one of the best professors you can get. Very professional, clean, reasonable, and passionate. He understands the structure of his lecture and class very clearly and teaches things in an intriguing way with great tips and lessons on how you can keep handling academic writing or study in the future as well.
He is honestly one of the best profs I have ever had. His lectures are entertaining, his assignments are easy if you keep up with the readings, and he has been super accomodating during the pandemic.

Genuinely wonderful. So funny & memorable. I can't recommend him enough. He made the online format SO doable: omitting some assignments, making tutorials asynchronous, giving clear learning aids, etc. While he does assign a lot of readings, they're truly enlightening, and he explains them in class. Just 1 short non-research paper & 2 easy MC exams.

HE IS THE BEST. If you haven't taken the class CA 136 yet, please do it. It's worth it. He is so passionate about his work and he explains everything so clearly. Not to mention he is very chill and funny. I took this course online during COVID and even though it was online it was still one of my favourite classes this semester. Should be mandatory.

Great guy. Understanding and chill. If you're having trouble with a concept or need an extension, just ask and he's happy to help. Sometimes I got bored in class but that was more to do with my short attention span than his teacher. He's a good pick. P.S. He may go on a rant about hating Tom Hanks once or twice!

Love Pavsek! His lectures were clear and to the point while also relating to both the textbook and film screenings. I felt like I actually learned in this class, instead of just getting credit. Plus his anecdotes are interesting and funny.

Chris is a hilarious professor in a very sarcastic way! His classes are very fun! We view a film nearly every week! Make sure you attend the classes, and take notes, since most of the midterm/final questions are from there! Do read the canvas readings, (they will be on the exam). We also had two 3 to 4 pages papers to write, so start early! Enjoy!

Almost every class we would watch a movie. Class not mandatory but if you miss one it's hard to catch up because there are no recordings. A great beginners course to film or arts in general. Really kind. I was only on the waiting list for this course until I talked to Chris and he personally helped me get in. Textbook readings not mandatory at all.

Chris is an awesome prof who always kept even the most confusing concepts in film history easy to understand. I loved how he was always upbeat, never alienated anyone, and tests were all super fair. Plus he's pretty easy on the eyes.

BEST class I've ever taken. All of Chris' film classes are excellent but this one is even better. It's clear that he knows what he's talking about and is very passionate about the theorists he discusses. He also gives some of the most helpful feedback on essays and proves that not all profs phone it in.

Chris is great, keeps things interesting and insightful. Strikes a good balance in teaching established film theories while also critically questioning their applicability and relevance. Exams are extremely fair. Great personality.
THE AMOUNT OF READINGS IS INSANE!!!!!

Chris is such a great prof! Real funny, keeps lectures very interesting! He makes 3 hour lectures a breeze! Lectures are a must attend because you NEED to see all the films/clips. Most of them are really old, so they're hard to find online. Memorize bold terms in Film Art. Oxford History not so important. PAY ATTENTION/STUDY THE WEBCT READINGS WELL

Unlike most profs, he's very clear in lectures. Pretty funny guy, makes things interesting. However WAY too much reading. Tests are all writing, unreasonable amount of time for the midterm. Don't skip tutorials or you risk failing. Don't take this if you don't like symbolism and deeply interpreting things.

Really fun class. Chris loves what he does and he's a really loveable prof. Not a hard class but must attend lectures or at least watch all the movies that are shown in the lectures. Exams are mostly based on those movies and the text. Tutorials are really helpful, attend them!

Really amazing class. Prof is hilarious/nice/and articulates all arguments perfectly.  Make sure you do the online readings and just know the bold words in the Film Art textbook. Didn't even open Oxford History.  TA's really know their stuff.  I took it as a Breadth requirement for my Bio degree and thoroughly enjoyed it.

He's a funny, articulate, and clear guy. Unforunately, the amount of reading assigned is impossible. Warning: the reading is due for the day of the lecture, and there is some for the first one. So, you will have 2 weeks of reading in the first week of class. I never recovered from this, even though I have an A/A-. Read the essays, forget the books.

WAY too much reading for a film course!!!! Or any course- it really is that bad. Chris is humourous, the course material is rich and very useful, except that the amount of reading required is ABSURD, and the tutorial a waste of time, even though the my TA is such a super-nice person.

His comments are often political and always humourous. I would sit in on another of his classes just because he's such a great speaker. I took this as an elective and have enjoyed the lectures; there is a lot of reading but you can do well by skimmming Film Art and WebCT readings and listening to summaries in tutorial. TA was disorganized.

His humour was the only half-decent part about this course. I completely agree w/ whoever said that Jaws was ruined b/c of the analysis. TAs and tutorials were completely useless and half of the movies were brutal to watch (I slept thru most of them).  Exams are not very easy. Definitely would not do another FPA course.

Chris is one of the greatest profs I have ever had the luck to take a class with. I took this for an elective, and had no idea what I was getting into, but his lectures are always interesting and entertaining. I will definitely take a class with him again.

Chris is really funny in an off beat sort of way.  I love the stuff he teaches because it is often very philosophical.  If you are looking for a history course I don't recommend FPA 136 or 137, but if you want to debate the theories of great french film critics this is the course for you!  Advice: focus on the articles NOT Oxford history.

Awesome prof, awesome class.  I thought it would be a slack class where I just got to watch movies for an easy A.  It was actually kind of challenging because there was a lot of reading.  He kept the lectures funny and entertaining and the movies got better and better.  Favourite class!

great prof,great class..he is funny and totally charming.The only thing i'd say is there's waaay too much reading and he talks really fast and sometimes it's hard to take notes.But the prof is wonderful and everytime i go to class i'm happy
Great class for someone who LOVES film and is interested in film theory...Chris definitely knows his stuff!!

a really nice guy. good prof, but not a great one. one reason: it's kinda hard to hear him in lecture cos he doesn't use mic, and his voice can be quite soft. other than that, i'd say it's fun to take his class.

Meh, class was ok. I was taking this class for PDP admissions. Some of the films were interesting but I ended up sleeping through a couple of them. Ruined Jaws for me with the anaylsis (...the shark actually was symbol of the repressed black man, give me a break). Readings are tough and extremely boring. Midterm was ok and tutorials alright.
Very funny, knowledgable prof. If you're a film buff, you should deffinitely take his class
He was pretty good! Of course, he's not an english teacher, so SFU shouldn't have had him teaching an english course, but for what he got thrown into, he did his best! GREAT film teacher
The worst english class i have ever taken...save yourselves from misery and stay away from this guy
